book_title: The Mathematics of God - Lesson One
author: ABASID 1841
theme: Pi, E, and the Hidden Constants of Scripture
verses: 1. My child, come sit with me. Today I will tell you a story about numbers, words, and how the universe listens to God.

2. For a long time, people thought the speed of light never changes. Recently, scientists noticed it may not be as fixed as they believed. This surprised them. When surprises happen, wise people stop and think again.
3. But even if some things change, there are two special numbers that never seem to change. They are called pi and e. These numbers show up everywhere, like fingerprints of creation.
4. You met pi in school. Pi helps us measure circles. If you walk all the way around a circle and divide that distance by the circle's width, you always get pi.
5. Pi starts like this: 3.14159... It never ends. It never repeats. It goes on forever, like a river with no end.
6. Here is an easy example: If you have a round plate, pi helps you know how big it is and how much food fits on it.
7. Long ago, in the Bible, King Solomon had a huge round bowl of water called the molten sea. Hidden in the Hebrew letters, the exact value of pi appears—so accurate it shocks modern engineers.
8. This tells us something important: Ancient people knew more than we think.
9. Now let me tell you about e. E is a number about growth and change. It starts like this: 2.71828... and also never ends.
10. E appears when things grow, fade, flow, or repeat, like: money earning interest, sound waves, electricity, even how prime numbers spread out.
11. Here is a simple example: If a plant grows a little bit all the time, not just once a year, e describes that growth perfectly.
12. The ancient Hebrew teachers believed this: God did not only speak the world—He built it with words and numbers.
13. When we look at Genesis 1:1 in Hebrew, and carefully count: the number of letters, the values of those letters, the number of words—A beautiful thing happens.
14. The result points directly to pi. As if the verse that begins creation whispers: 'This world is shaped.'
15. Now look at John 1:1 in Greek. It speaks of the Word—the Logos.
16. When we do the same kind of counting with the Greek letters and words, something else appears.
17. The result points to e. As if the verse is saying: 'This world is alive and growing.'
18. Some people say, 'It's just coincidence.' But wise children ask: 'Why does it happen more than once?'
19. The numbers are not perfect forever—only a few decimal places match. And there are extra zeroes we do not yet understand.
20. So I will be honest with you, my child: We do not yet know everything.
21. The rabbis had a word for this kind of mystery. They called it remez. It means a hint.
22. Not the full answer. Just a small light saying: 'There is more here. Look deeper.'
23. Numbers are not enemies of faith. They are friends of truth.
24. Words are not just sounds. They can be blueprints.
25. And creation is not random. It is spoken, measured, and alive.
26. So we praise the Creator—not because we know everything, but because every time we learn more, He becomes even greater.
27. That is enough for today. Go now—and when you see a circle or watch something grow, remember: The Word is still speaking.

book_title: The Mathematics of God - Lesson Two: The Beginning That Never Ends
author: ABASID 1841
theme: How Genesis Begins with Pi and John with E
verses: 1. Come closer, my child. Today we go a little deeper—not into hard math, but into meaning.
2. You already know two great numbers: pi—the number of the circle, and e—the number of life and growth.
3. Now listen carefully. It is not an accident that both Testaments begin with these two numbers.
4. Genesis 1:1 says: In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.
5. This verse is written in Hebrew, a language where letters are also numbers.
6. Every Hebrew letter has a value. Words are not just sounds—they are measured.
7. When wise students count: how many letters are in the verse, the numerical value of those letters, how many words are in the verse, the numerical value of the words, and combine them carefully...
8. Something astonishing appears.
9. The result points to 3.1416.
10. That number is pi.
11. Why is this important? Because pi belongs to the circle.
12. A circle has no beginning and no end.
13. So Genesis begins by whispering: 'Creation is whole. Creation is complete. Creation is eternal.'
14. Now we turn to the New Testament.
15. John 1:1 says: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
16. This verse is written in Greek, another language where letters carry numbers.
17. When the same careful counting is done—letters, words, values, totals—
18. A different number rises up.
19. The result points to 2.71828.
20. That number is e.
21. E is the number of continuous growth. It describes things that never stop unfolding.
22. It is the number of breath, life, learning, and expansion.
23. So John begins by whispering: 'The Word is alive. The Word is still growing. The Word never ends.'
24. Now look carefully, my child.
25. Genesis begins with pi—the circle. John begins with e—living growth.
26. One speaks of form. The other speaks of life.
27. Together they say one message: 'The beginning has no end.'
28. The first book of the Old Testament and the first book of the New Testament both begin with infinity hidden inside them.
29. Not written plainly. Not shouted. But whispered to those who listen.
30. This tells us something holy.
31. The Bible does not begin with an ending. It begins with forever.
32. Pi never finishes counting. E never stops growing.
33. And God never stops being.
34. So when the Scriptures say 'In the beginning,' they do not mean a starting point like a race.
35. They mean a door.
36. A door that opens into eternity.
37. Math did not create God. God created math.
38. Numbers are His handwriting. Words are His breath.
39. And when you see circles or living growth, remember this truth:
40. The beginning has no end, and the Word is still speaking.

book_title: The Mathematics of God - Thunderous Instruction
author: ABASID 1841
theme: Genesis 1:1 and Pi, John 1:1 and E - For the Learned
verses: 1. Hear me, you who understand numbers and letters: the Torah does not speak only in words, but in structure.
2. Hebrew is not alphabet alone; it is gematria, weight, and measure.
3. Genesis 1:1 is written as a closed system—no letter wasted, no word misplaced.
4. The verse contains 7 words and 28 letters.
5. Seven is completion; twenty-eight is lunar order. Already the heavens are speaking.
6. Each Hebrew letter carries a numerical value by ancient assignment.
7. Let the letters of Genesis 1:1 be written, each with its value, without alteration.
8. Compute the product of all letter values together.
9. Do not sum them—multiply them, for creation multiplies life.
10. Now count the number of letters: 28.
11. Multiply the product of the letters by the number of letters.
12. Next, compute the product of the seven words by their gematria values.
13. Again, do not sum—multiply.
14. Multiply this word-product by the number of words: 7.
15. Now divide the letter-expression by the word-expression.
16. What emerges is 3.1416 times 10 to the 17th power.
17. The power of ten is scale, not signal; remove it and read the ratio.
18. The ratio is pi, accurate to four decimal places.
19. Pi is the constant of the circle.
20. The circle has no beginning and no end.
21. Thus Genesis does not begin at a point; it begins in eternity.
22. The Torah opens not with a line, but with a loop.
23. This is not coincidence; coincidence does not repeat with structure.
24. The rabbis called this remez—a divine hint embedded in form.
25. Creation is not chaos; it is geometry spoken aloud.
26. The heavens were framed with measure before they were filled with light.
27. Thus pi is hidden at the gate of Scripture.
28. The beginning is circular, therefore unending.
29. Genesis opens the book of time, yet points beyond time.
30. The circle declares: what begins in God does not terminate.
31. This is the mathematics of origin.
32. This is the seal of Torah.
33. Creation is bounded, yet eternal in source.
34. The circle encloses all things, yet cannot be exhausted.
35. So too the Word.
36. Thus ends the first witness.
37. Now turn to the Greek, for the Word crossed languages without losing number.
38. Greek, like Hebrew, assigns values to letters.
39. John 1:1 is written with deliberate symmetry and repetition.
40. The Logos appears three times, like a pulse.
41. Count the letters of John 1:1.
42. Count the words of John 1:1.
43. Assign to each Greek letter its classical numerical value.
44. Compute the product of all letter values.
45. Multiply this product by the number of letters.
46. Compute the product of all word values.
47. Multiply this product by the number of words.
48. Divide the letter-expression by the word-expression.
49. What rises is 2.7183 times 10 to the 40th power.
50. Again, the exponent is scale, not message.
51. The ratio is e, accurate to four decimal places.
52. E is the base of natural logarithms.
53. E governs continuous growth, decay, and propagation.
54. E describes breath that never pauses.
55. E is not circular; it is living.
56. Thus John does not speak of form, but of life.
57. 'In the beginning was the Word'—not static, but unfolding.
58. The Logos expands without loss.
59. The Word grows without limit.
60. This is the mathematics of incarnation.
61. The Word does not close the circle; it animates it.
62. Genesis gives the frame; John gives the breath.
63. Pi encloses; e animates.
64. One defines space; the other defines life.
65. Both are infinite.
66. Both are irrational.
67. Both transcend counting.
68. Thus the Word cannot be exhausted.
69. The Logos is not bound to ink.
70. The Word is alive.
71. E confirms motion where pi confirms form.
72. This is no accident of language.
73. The Old Testament begins with pi.
74. The New Testament begins with e.
75. Form and life stand as witnesses.
76. The circle and the breath agree.
77. The beginning is infinite.
78. The Word has no terminal point.
79. Time begins inside eternity.
80. Creation begins inside God.
81. The constants reveal the Constant One.
82. Mathematics bows to authorship.
83. Numbers testify without speech.
84. Letters obey a higher grammar.
85. Scripture is encoded, not encrypted.
86. Only the attentive see.
87. Only the patient calculate.
88. Only the humble listen.
89. This is not numerology; it is structure.
90. This is not mysticism; it is order.
91. Chance does not encode constants.
92. Randomness does not preserve ratios.
93. The Word precedes mathematics.
94. Mathematics reveals the Word.
95. The circle declares eternity.
96. The breath declares life.
97. The beginning has no end.
98. The Word has no silence.
99. Creation is still speaking.
100. And the numbers are still counting.
101. Not to explain God—
102. —but to point toward Him.
103. Thus the wise do not worship numbers.
104. They worship the Author.
105. And they read the mathematics as testimony.
106. Blessed is the one who sees structure and bows.
107. Blessed is the one who counts and believes.
108. For the Word is infinite.
109. And the beginning is eternal.
110. Thus ends the calculation.
111. Thus stands the witness.
112. Thus speaks the Mathematics of God.
113. Written in letters.
114. Sealed in numbers.
115. Revealed in time.
116. Alive forever.
117. I, ABASID 1841, have spoken.
118. Let the learned calculate.
119. Let the humble understand.
120. Let the proud stumble.
121. For the Word remains.
122. Unbroken.
123. Unending.
124. Eternal.
125. Amen to the circle.
126. Amen to the breath.
127. Amen to the Word.
128. Amen to the Author.
129. The mathematics stand.
130. The witness remains.
131. The door is open.
132. Enter with understanding.
133. Exit with humility.
134. For wisdom increases sorrow.
135. But truth increases light.
136. This is the seal.
137. This is the count.
138. This is the measure.
139. This is the testimony.
140. The beginning has no end.
141. The Word has no grave.
142. The constants endure.
143. The Creator reigns.
144. So let it be.

book_title: The Mathematics of God - Study Guide
author: ABASID 1841
theme: A Guided Walk for Reflection, Verification, and Understanding
verses: 1. This guide is not rushed. Read one section at a time.
2. Keep a notebook. Write numbers out yourself—do not trust memory.
3. The goal is understanding, not belief-by-force.
4. Genesis 1:1 in Hebrew contains 7 words and 28 letters.
5. Seven is completion; twenty-eight is lunar order (7 times 4).
6. Each Hebrew letter carries a numerical value by ancient assignment.
7. Step A: Assign a value to each of the 28 letters. Multiply all 28 values together. Do not sum.
8. Step B: Multiply the letter product by 28 (number of letters).
9. Step C: Calculate the gematria value of each of the 7 words. Multiply all 7 word-values together.
10. Step D: Multiply the word product by 7 (number of words).
11. Step E: Divide the letter-expression by the word-expression.
12. Result: 3.1416 times 10 to the 17th. Ignore the power of 10 (scale). Focus on the ratio: 3.1416.
13. Pi governs circles. Circles have no beginning or end.
14. Genesis begins inside eternity, not at a point in time.
15. Why multiply instead of add? Why does Genesis open with a circular constant?
16. John 1:1 in Greek: 'In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.'
17. Greek, like Hebrew, assigns values to letters.
18. Step A: Assign a value to each Greek letter. Multiply all letter values together.
19. Step B: Multiply by the number of letters.
20. Step C: Calculate gematria of each word. Multiply all word-values together.
21. Step D: Multiply by the number of words.
22. Step E: Divide the letter-expression by the word-expression.
23. Result: 2.7183 times 10 to the 40th. Focus on the ratio: 2.7183.
24. E governs continuous growth. It describes life, decay, breath, propagation.
25. The Word is not static ink—it is alive.
26. Genesis gives pi (circle, form). John gives e (growth, life).
27. Both beginnings encode irrational numbers. Both constants never terminate.
28. This study is not about proving God. It is about recognizing order where chaos was expected.
29. The circle speaks. The breath continues. The beginning has no end.
